title: Nested Card Component
description: Write nested CSS for a card with title, description, and featured modifier.
hint: Use & for the featured modifier and nest .title and .desc inside.
solution: .card { background: #1a1a2e; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; .title { color: #ffd700; font-size: 1.2rem; } .desc { color: #94a3b8; } &.featured { border-color: #3b82f6; .title { color: #3b82f6; } } }

title: Nested Button with Hover
description: Write nested CSS for a button with :hover and .outline modifier.
solution: .btn { padding: 12px 24px; background: #3b82f6; color: white; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; &:hover { background: #1d4ed8; } &.outline { background: transparent; border: 2px solid #3b82f6; &:hover { background: #3b82f6; color: white; } } }

title: Nested Layout with Media Query
description: Create a grid layout that changes to single column on mobile using nested @media.
solution: .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 16px; @media (max-width: 768px) { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }

title: Nested Navigation with Pseudo-Elements
description: Build a navigation component with nested styles including ::after underline effect and active state.
hint: Use &::after for underline and &.active for current page.
solution: .nav { display: flex; gap: 24px; a { color: #e0e0e0; text-decoration: none; position: relative; &::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: -4px; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 2px; background: #3b82f6; transform: scaleX(0); transition: transform 0.3s; } &:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); } &.active { color: #3b82f6; } } }
